title: eremophila glabra subsp. albicans
intro: Eremophila glabra subsp. albicans, also known as the white-leafed emu bush, is a subspecies of the emu bush plant native to Western Australia. It has been traditionally used in Australian bush medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and soothing skin conditions. Eremophila glabra subsp. albicans can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture for medicinal use, although its effectiveness and safety as a treatment should be verified by medical professionals.
